examples.note: a title, 4 tokens → fused: BM25 over the words ⊕ semantic neighbours, merged by RRF. Tolkien, top to bottom.
describe it, don't title it → fused (5 tokens): keyword leg catches 'ring', semantic leg pulls the Lord of the Rings volumes to the top.

examples.text: a quest to destroy a magic ring
examples.note: 5 tokens → fused: no title words to match, so the semantic leg carries it, clustering epic-fantasy quests with The Lord of the Rings among them.
5 tokens → fused: BM25 nails the title words, semantic keeps the rest of the series close. Top hit: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets.
describe the vibe → fused (3 tokens): keyword leg on 'wizard'/'story' ⊕ semantic on the idea. Top hit: a book literally titled Wizard.
misspelled, 2 tokens → hybrid_text; the fuzzy legs still find Frodo Baggins and the LOTR volumes through the typo.
misspelled, 2 tokens → hybrid_text; the fuzzy legs still find the Harry Potter series through the typo.

examples.text: an orphan boy who discovers he is a wizard and attends a school for magic
examples.note: a full sentence, 13 tokens → semantic: ANN over descriptions, no title words needed. Returns orphan-wizard stories, Harry Potter among them.
